title: Fragments of Light III
description: The third work in Marchetti's celebrated Fragments series, this luminous canvas explores the dissolution of form through layered glazes of translucent oil. Shafts of golden light fracture across the surface, creating a meditative tension between order and entropy. A quietly commanding presence in any space.

title: Portobello Morning
description: Whitfield captures the amber stillness of a Notting Hill morning before the market crowds arrive, the cobblestones slick with overnight rain. The palette — ochre, sage, and weathered terracotta — distils a familiar London scene into something timeless. One of the artist's most sought-after original works.

title: Study in Vermillion
description: A bold investigation of colour as both subject and structure, this square-format canvas pulses with layers of cadmium red and raw umber applied with deliberate, gestural precision. Marchetti's restraint is as notable as her daring — the composition breathes despite its intensity. A defining work from her 2023 studio period.

title: The Weight of Silence
description: Osei's portraiture operates in the liminal space between presence and introspection, and this haunting panel is among his most emotionally direct works. The subject's downcast gaze and beautifully rendered skin tones speak of interiority, dignity, and quiet power. Executed in traditional oil glazes with extraordinary technical mastery.

title: Golden Hour, Hackney
description: Whitfield turns his eye east, finding unexpected beauty in the industrial rooflines and canal paths of Hackney at dusk. The watercolour medium lends the piece an ethereal softness that transforms the urban into the almost pastoral. Framed and presented in an archival mount, ready to hang.

title: Bloom IV
description: The fourth in Laurent's acclaimed Bloom series, this mixed-media work layers encaustic wax, oil pastel, and pressed botanical elements into a surface of extraordinary richness and depth. The flowers seem to exist somewhere between life and memory, between botanical study and pure abstraction. A singular work full of quiet exuberance.

title: Urban Archaeology
description: Chen excavates the visual language of the urban environment, layering stencilled figures, fragmented type, and gestural spray work into a composition that reads like a compressed archive of city life. The reclaimed board substrate adds genuine texture and history to the piece. One of eight signed and numbered editions.

title: Dissolution No. 7
description: Working in the ancient medium of encaustic wax, Marchetti creates surfaces of astonishing luminosity and complexity. Layers of pigmented wax are fused and incised to reveal strata of colour beneath, producing a work of considerable meditative depth. The square format gives Dissolution No. 7 an icon-like authority.

title: The Listener
medium: Charcoal and soft pastel on paper
description: Rendered with breathtaking economy of line, this charcoal and pastel work captures a figure in an attitude of attentive stillness. Osei's mastery of tone and subtle colour allows him to suggest an entire interior world with a handful of marks. Presented framed under museum glass.

title: Meridian
description: Novak's Meridian is a study in dynamic equilibrium — a bronze form that appears simultaneously to fall and to rise, caught in a perpetual moment of becoming. The rich, hand-applied patina moves from deep sienna at the base to a cool verdigris at the apex. Cast in an edition of eight, each piece individually finished by the artist.

title: Peonies in January
description: Against the austere blue-grey of a winter studio, Laurent's peonies are an act of quiet defiance — overgrown, extravagant, and fully alive. The artist's handling of petals in oil is virtuosic, each bloom a study in controlled abandon. A beautiful and deeply romantic original work.

title: Strata Blue
description: Chen moves from street to studio in this refined mixed-media work, deploying collaged cartography, ink, and acrylic in geological layers of blue and grey. The result suggests depth, history, and the invisible structures that underlie any city. A sophisticated and collectible piece from a rising talent.
